sections.paragraph: ἀϰροβυστία, ας, ἡ [ἄϰρος ‘highest’, πόσϑη ‘penis’ a variation of ποσϑία ‘foreskin’] ‘prepuce of the penis’, foreskin Ac 11:3 (to have a foreskin means that one is uncircumcised and outside the Mosaic cultic tradition); sim. the metaphorical usage Ro 2:25, 26, 27. Also metaphorical of behavior predating Christian experience Col 2:13; of those who are outside the Mosaic cultic tradition non-circumcised, non-Judeans, gentiles Ro 3:30 and oft. in Ro; non-divisive aspect Col 3:11.
